content.text:
<user_query>
Run my backend - Backend Setup
cd backend
composer install
copy config.example.php config.php
Edit .env with your SMTP credentials (Gmail App Password recommended).

Run PHP Server (Development)
From project root:

php -S localhost:8000
</user_query>

I'll set up and start the backend: checking the project structure and any running servers first.

[REDACTED]

input.command: where.exe php 2>$null; if (Test-Path "C:\xampp\php\php.exe") { "C:\xampp\php\php.exe" }; if (Test-Path "C:\laragon\bin\php") { Get-ChildItem "C:\laragon\bin\php" -Recurse -Filter php.exe | Select-Object -First 3 -ExpandProperty FullName }; Get-Command php -ErrorAction SilentlyContinue | Select-Object -ExpandProperty Source

where.exe composer 2>$null; if (Test-Path "C:\xampp\php\composer.phar") { "composer.phar in xampp" }; if (Test-Path "C:\ProgramData\ComposerSetup\bin\composer.bat") { "C:\ProgramData\ComposerSetup\bin\composer.bat" }; Get-ChildItem -Path "C:\Users\bhara" -Filter composer.phar -Recurse -Depth 4 -ErrorAction SilentlyContinue | Select-Object -First 3 -ExpandProperty FullName

cd "e:\MC-Projects\MKS-loom" && git log --all --oneline -- "**/start.bat" "**/router.php" 2>$null | Select-Object -First 5; git show HEAD:backend/start.bat 2>$null; git ls-files | Select-String -Pattern "start|router|api"
input.description: Check git history for start.bat and routing

input.description: Start PHP dev server on port 8000

input.command: try { $r = Invoke-WebRequest -Uri "http://localhost:8000/api/enquiry.php" -Method OPTIONS -UseBasicParsing; "OPTIONS status: $($r.StatusCode)" } catch { "OPTIONS error: $($_.Exception.Message)" }; try { $body = '{"name":"Test","phone":"9876543210","email":"test@example.com","product":"Test Product","message":"Hello"}'; $r = Invoke-WebRequest -Uri "http://localhost:8000/api/enquiry.php" -Method POST -ContentType "application/json" -Body $body -UseBasicParsing; "POST status: $($r.StatusCode)"; $r.Content } catch { if ($_.Exception.Response) { $reader = [System.IO.StreamReader]::new($_.Exception.Response.GetResponseStream()); "POST error status: $([int]$_.Exception.Response.StatusCode)"; $reader.ReadToEnd() } else { "POST error: $($_.Exception.Message)" } }

content.text:
Your backend is up and running. Here’s what was done:

### Setup completed

1. **`composer install`** — PHP and Composer aren’t on your system PATH, so dependencies were installed with XAMPP’s PHP:
   - `C:\xampp\php\php.exe composer.phar install`
   - Installed `phpmailer/phpmailer` v6.12.0 into `backend/vendor/`

2. **`config.php`** — Already present (no `config.example.php` in the repo).

3. **`.env`** — Created from `.env.example` and filled with SMTP settings from your existing `config.php`, including local dev CORS origins for Vite (`localhost:5173`).

4. **PHP server** — Started from the `backend` folder (same as your old `start.bat`):
   - **URL:** http://localhost:8000  
   - **API:** http://localhost:8000/api/enquiry.php  

### Verified

A test `POST` to the enquiry endpoint returned `200` with a success response.

### Notes

- **`php` and `composer` aren’t in PATH** in your terminal. To run them directly, either add `C:\xampp\php` to PATH, or use the full path: `C:\xampp\php\php.exe`.
- The README says to run `php -S localhost:8000` from the **project root**, but the API lives under `backend/api/`. The server needs to run with `backend` as the document root (from the `backend` folder, or `php -S localhost:8000 -t backend` from the root).
- The Vite dev server proxies `/api` → `http://localhost:8000`, so the frontend should work with this setup.

The server is running in the background. Stop it with Ctrl+C in that terminal when you’re done.

[REDACTED]
